_doc: See docs/adr/0001-roles-and-approval-design.md before adding/renaming roles. Roles drive permission gates; approval routing lives in a separate per-employee chain (lands with leave module). Do NOT invent a parallel approval system here.
name: SUPER_ADMIN
description: Bypasses all permission checks via system_admin flag. Reserved for ops/infra.

name: HR_ADMIN
description: HR back office. Manages users, sees roles + permissions, manages time entries and work schedules for any employee. Holds the ApproveAny override for leave + time-correction; never sits ON a chain (no Approve / Create).

name: OPERATIONS_MANAGER
description: Bridges HR and PM. Read access to people and roles for staffing visibility. Chain placement determines whether they approve a specific request (ADR 0001). Q7 (2026-05-30): no leave / correction / chain admin grants in v1.

name: PROJECT_MANAGER
description: 2nd-level approver in employee leave chains. Authority over a specific request requires being on that employee's approval chain (see ADR 0001). PM/TD distinction lives in chain placement, not in role permissions.

name: TECHNICAL_DIRECTOR
description: 1st-level approver in employee leave chains. Identical permission set to PROJECT_MANAGER; the PM/TD distinction lives in the approval chain step (see ADR 0001).

name: EMPLOYEE
description: Default role for new registrations. Self-service routes only — can submit leave + correction requests and see their own.
